ID:       ddx-ccdf9cf9
Title:    test-infra: e2e suite drifted from node/project router; needs Go-server harness + seeded fixture
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:test, area:frontend, kind:test-infra, phase:test
Owner:    ddx
Deps:     ddx-fc6e5b14, ddx-9f8092f1, ddx-0724aaba, ddx-c2860348, ddx-d15b6aa1, ddx-ae4e467b, ddx-b06a2f9f
Desc:     ## Problem

`bun run test:e2e` from `cli/internal/server/frontend` exits 1: 42 of 196 tests fail, 149 pass, 5 skipped. The failures are not regressions — they are accumulated drift between the test suite and the current UI. `playwright.config.ts` runs only `bun run preview` (port 4173, static SvelteKit output, no Go backend), and the suite outgrew that scope.

Bead ddx-955b9c41 (closed 2026-04-21) flagged the split between `playwright.config.ts` (4173) and `playwright.demo.config.ts` (18080) as "likely intentional but undocumented". This epic resolves it.

## Failure breakdown

**(A) TC-008 HTTP API — 6 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:368-409`. Direct `request.get('/api/...')` to `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, `/api/docs/graph`. Static preview returns 404; the Go server returns 200 for all six (verified with curl against the running ddx-server on https://127.0.0.1:7743 during the session that filed this bead).

**(B) TC-001 Dashboard — 8 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:9-57`. All visit `/` and expect `h1` "Dashboard". Per Stage 3.8 (commit 3bf655cf), `src/routes/+page.svelte` does `goto(`/nodes/${nodeStore.value.id}`, { replaceState: true })` whenever a node is selected — `/` is no longer a Dashboard route. Tests were not migrated.

**(C) Pages requiring backend data — ~28 tests.** Spread across `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007), `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` (TC-006, TC-007), `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts`. All fail because the static preview can't serve real document/bead/session data.

## In-scope files

Configuration:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — switch `webServer` to spawn the Go ddx server (built binary or `go run`) on a deterministic port with a fixture working dir, instead of `bun run preview`. Set `use.baseURL` to that port. Keep `bun run preview` available as a separate project ("static-only") for any tests that genuinely don't need a backend.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts` — **leave alone**. Used for demo recording; do not modify.

Fixture:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/<name>/` — new directory containing a seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (handful of beads in varied states: open, closed, ready, blocked), a tiny `docs/` library (a few documents the API can index), and any minimum config the server needs. Self-contained — must NOT depend on the developer's `~/.config/ddx` or the parent repo's `.ddx/`.

Tests (categorize and migrate):
- `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, TC-008) — rewrite navigation paths to use `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, matching the pattern in `e2e/navigation.spec.ts:177`. Drop assumptions about `/` resolving to a Dashboard route.
- `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts`, `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — verify each passes against the new harness; update navigation paths and any data-shape assumptions where needed.

CI / runbook:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or whatever is canonical) — document that `bun run test:e2e` now requires the ddx Go binary built first, and how the fixture is structured.

## Out-of-scope (file separate beads if needed)

- Server source changes in `cli/internal/server/` — the API is correct; this bead is about test wiring.
- Frontend route additions or component changes — only test files and fixtures change.
- `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `demo-recording.spec.ts` semantics — leave untouched (recording is its own concern).
- Visual regression baselines in `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` — re-baselining if pixel diffs appear is a follow-up bead, not this one.

## Why an epic, not a single task

Per beads.md, anything that touches more than ~3 files or crosses subsystems should decompose. This crosses test-infra (playwright config + fixture seeding) and four distinct test files at minimum. Codex should decompose during refinement: at minimum, "(1) harness + fixture", "(2) migrate TC-001/TC-008", "(3) migrate page-data tests", and "(4) update runbook".

## Rollback / cleanup

# DDx Frontend

SvelteKit frontend for the DDx server UI. Built with Bun, Tailwind CSS, Vitest, and Playwright.

## Setup

```sh
bun install
```

## Development

Start the dev server on http://localhost:5173:

```sh
bun run dev
```

## Building

Build for production (outputs to `build/`):

```sh
bun run build
```

Preview the production build:

```sh
bun run preview
```

## Testing

Run unit tests (Vitest):

```sh
bun run test:unit
```

Run end-to-end tests (Playwright):

```sh
bun run test:e2e
```

Run all tests:

```sh
bun run test
```

### Fixture-backed e2e harness

The default Playwright config (`playwright.config.ts`) boots a real `ddx server`
against a self-contained fixture workspace, so backend-dependent specs hit live
API endpoints without reading `$HOME`, `~/.config/ddx`, or the repository's live
`.ddx/` state.

What `bun run test:e2e` does on each run:

1. Copies `e2e/fixtures/` into a fresh `mktemp -d` workspace.
2. Runs `go build` from the `cli/` module to produce a `ddx` binary inside that
   temp dir. You do **not** need to pre-build or install `ddx` — Playwright's
   `webServer` builds it for you. (`go run` is not used; the harness execs the
   built binary so startup is fast and reproducible.) The Go toolchain must be
   on `PATH`.
3. Execs `./ddx server --tsnet=false --addr=127.0.0.1 --port=4174` from the
   temp workspace. Tailscale (tsnet) is disabled, and because the temp dir has
   no `.git`, `FindProjectRoot` falls back to it — the server sees only the
   fixture.

Override the port with `DDX_E2E_PORT` if 4174 is taken.

#### Fixture location and layout

The fixture lives at `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/` and contains:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so bead endpoints return

What `bun run test:e2e` does on each run:

1. Copies `e2e/fixtures/` into a fresh `mktemp -d` workspace.
2. Runs `go build` from the `cli/` module to produce a `ddx` binary inside that
   temp dir. You do **not** need to pre-build or install `ddx` — Playwright's
   `webServer` builds it for you. (`go run` is not used; the harness execs the
   built binary so startup is fast and reproducible.) The Go toolchain must be
   on `PATH`.
3. Execs `./ddx server --tsnet=false --addr=127.0.0.1 --port=4174` from the
   temp workspace. Tailscale (tsnet) is disabled, and because the temp dir has
   no `.git`, `FindProjectRoot` falls back to it — the server sees only the
   fixture.

Override the port with `DDX_E2E_PORT` if 4174 is taken.

#### Fixture location and layout

The fixture lives at `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/` and contains:

- `.ddx/config.yaml` — minimal DDx config pointing at the local plugin library.
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` — open, closed, and blocked beads so bead endpoints return
  non-empty data.
- `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` — minimal personas, prompts, and templates so document
  and persona endpoints have something to list.
- `docs/` — small docs library for the document graph endpoint.

The fixture is treated as read-only: the harness copies it before boot, so test
runs never mutate the checked-in files.

#### Extending the fixture

When a new spec needs additional backend data, add it under
`e2e/fixtures/` as static files — never reach into `$HOME`, `~/.config/ddx`, or
the repository's own `.ddx/`. Typical extensions:

- More beads → append JSONL records to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- New personas, prompts, or templates → add files under
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/{personas,prompts,templates}/`.
- New docs for the document graph → add Markdown under `docs/`.

Keep additions minimal and self-contained; the fixture is the only supported
data source for the e2e suite. Developer-local DDx config is intentionally not
part of this harness.

> Note: `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts` are a
> separate demo-recording configuration with its own setup — it is not the
> fixture-backed e2e harness described here.
